<!-- docgen:version=0.1.1 reason: cosmetic refresh; same single-file pre-push hook, no structural changes -->

## Purpose
Holds the `pre-push` git hook template that docgen installs into a clone so every user push triggers a detached, background README refresh. It's the one piece of plumbing that keeps generated READMEs from drifting after merges.

## Files
- `pre-push` — bash pre-push hook (marker `DOCGEN_PRE_PUSH_HOOK_v1`) that exits 0 immediately, self-re-execs detached via `setsid`/`nohup`, sleeps 5s for the user's push to settle, then runs `tools/docgen/docgen --until-done --parallel 4`, commits any README/`.docgen/state.json` deltas, and pushes them back with `DOCGEN_HOOK_SKIP=1`; on a deleted upstream branch it falls back to creating a fresh `docgen/refresh-<ts>` branch and opening a follow-up PR via `gh pr create --base main`. Logs to `/tmp/docgen-push-hook.log`.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- `tools/docgen/docgen` — the actual analyzer invoked by the background worker.
- `tools/docgen/install-push-hook.sh` — installer that detects the `DOCGEN_PRE_PUSH_HOOK_v1` marker before overwriting, and copies this file into `.git/hooks/pre-push`.
- `.docgen/state.json` — staged alongside README changes on the auto-commit.
- `gh` CLI — required only for the merged-branch fallback path; absent `gh`, the doc commit stays local.

## Gotchas
- Loop prevention hinges on `DOCGEN_HOOK_SKIP=1`: the hook noops at the top when set, and the background worker sets it before its own `git push` / `git checkout -b`. Removing either side reintroduces an infinite push loop.
- The hook intentionally `cat > /dev/null` drains stdin — pre-push hooks block until stdin is consumed, even though the ref list isn't used here.
- It runs `git add -A '*README.md' '.docgen/state.json'` from the repo root after docgen finishes; if a user has unrelated dirty README edits in the working tree at push time, they'll get swept into the auto-commit.
